circular.title: Master Circular on Board of Directors - UCBs
circular.summary: This Master Circular consolidates guidelines on the constitution, role, and responsibilities of the Board of Directors in Urban Co-operative Banks. It requires a minimum level of professionalism on the Board and defines a clear separation between policy oversight and day-to-day administration. The circular also prohibits non-statutory honorary titles and limits donations to trusts where directors have interests.

clauses.text: Primary (urban) co-operative banks (UCBs or banks) function under the regulation and supervision of RBI under the BR Act, 1949 and RBI Act, 1934.
clauses.summary: UCBs are regulated by RBI under the BR Act and RBI Act

clauses.text: The directors on the boards of UCBs must be knowledgeable and persons of high integrity.
clauses.summary: Directors must be knowledgeable and of high integrity

clauses.text: UCBs (other than those having deposit size less than Rs.100 crore and salary earners’ banks) are also required to constitute a Board of Management (BoM) in accordance with the guidelines contained in RBI circular dated December 31, 2019.
clauses.summary: UCBs must constitute a Board of Management

clauses.text: Persons engaged in money lending, financing and investment activities (individual or via concern) or those convicted of criminal offences including moral turpitude are ineligible to be directors.
UCBs are directed not to create any honorary positions/titles at Board level or confer such titles that are non-statutory in nature (e.g., Chairman Emeritus, Group Chairman).

The Board of Directors (BOD or Board) is primarily concerned with formulation of policies and exercise of overall supervision and control. Day-to-day administration must be left to the Managing Director (MD) / Chief Executive Officer (CEO).
clauses.summary: Board is responsible for policy and oversight

clauses.text: It should be ensured that all circulars and other material relating to policies issued by the RBI are placed before the Board for information and appropriate action.
clauses.summary: RBI circulars must be placed before the Board

clauses.text: Directors of UCBs are advised to be guided by several guidelines:

An apex Audit Committee (ACB) should be set up at the Board level. It may consist of a chairman and three/four directors, with one or more being chartered accountants or having experience in management, finance, accountancy, and audit.
clauses.summary: Audit Committee must be set up

clauses.text: The ACB should review RBI guideline implementation and submit a note to the Board quarterly. Major duties include: Providing direction and oversight to the entire audit function, Reviewing internal inspection/audit function systems and follow-up, Compliance with statutory/concurrent/RBI audit reports, Reviewing accounting policies for transparency and adequacy.
UCBs having asset size of ₹5000 crore or above (as on March 31 of the previous year) are advised to set up a Risk Management Committee (of the Board).
clauses.summary: Risk Management Committee must be set up

clauses.text: Directors should bestow attention on periodical reviews. An illustrative list with periodicity is provided in Annex 2.
UCBs are prohibited from giving donations to trusts and institutions where directors and/or their relatives hold a position or are interested, even within the 1% profit ceiling.

Relatives are defined as members of a Hindu Undivided Family, husband and wife, or specified relations (Father, Mother, Son, Son's wife, Daughter, Daughter's husband, Brother, Brother's wife, Sister, Sister's husband).
All expenses on Board meetings must be shown against P&L Account item 3 ("Directors and Local Committee Members – Fees and Allowance").

obligations.text: Maintain at least two professional directors with middle/senior banking experience or qualifications in law/finance/accountancy.
obligations.plain_english: Ensure at least two directors have relevant experience or qualifications

obligations.text: Constitute a Board of Management (BoM) for professional oversight of banking activities.
obligations.plain_english: Set up a Board of Management for oversight

obligations.text: Eliminate and refrain from creating non-statutory honorary titles like 'Chairman Emeritus' or 'Group Chairman'.
Ensure all RBI policy circulars are placed before the Board for information and action.
obligations.plain_english: Place RBI circulars before the Board

obligations.text: Establish an Audit Committee of the Board (ACB) to oversee internal audit and compliance.
Establish a Risk Management Committee of the Board.
obligations.plain_english: Set up a Risk Management Committee

obligations.text: Prohibit donations to trusts/institutions where directors or their relatives hold positions or are interested.
obligations.plain_english: Do not donate to trusts with director interests

obligations.text: Ensure directors do not participate in Board discussions where they have a direct or indirect interest.
obligations.plain_english: Directors must not participate in discussions with conflicts of interest

obligations.text: Submit periodic reviews (Monthly, Quarterly, Half-yearly, Annual) to the Board as per the specified calendar.
obligations.plain_english: Submit periodic reviews to the Board

prohibitions.text: UCBs are directed not to create any honorary positions/titles at Board level or confer such titles that are non-statutory in nature (e.g., Chairman Emeritus, Group Chairman).
UCBs are prohibited from giving donations to trusts and institutions where directors and/or their relatives hold a position or are interested, even within the 1% profit ceiling.
prohibitions.plain_english: Do not donate to trusts with director interests

definitions.definition: Directors having suitable banking experience at a middle/senior management level or relevant professional qualifications in law, accountancy, or finance.
definitions.plain_english: Directors with relevant experience or qualifications

definitions.definition: A professional body constituted within the bank (for UCBs over Rs. 100 crore deposits) to oversee banking-related activities, separate from the primary BOD.
definitions.plain_english: A committee to oversee banking activities

definitions.definition: Members of a Hindu Undivided Family, husband and wife, or specified relations (Father, Mother, Son, Son's wife, Daughter, Daughter's husband, Brother, Brother's wife, Sister, Sister's husband).
